skill.name: remotion-render
skill.description: Render React and Remotion component code into MP4 videos through the inference.sh belt CLI. The skill documents input options for TSX code, props, resolution, FPS, duration, and codec settings.
skill.summary: Render Remotion TSX components into MP4 videos using inference.sh.
skill.author: halt-catch-fire

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many Markdown backticks as shell execution and reported weak cryptography, but the reviewed SKILL.md contains documentation, examples, and no cryptographic implementation. The confirmed risk is that the skill intentionally uses the external inference.sh belt CLI to submit user-provided Remotion TSX and props for remote rendering. No prompt injection attempt, credential exfiltration instruction, or malicious intent was found.

medium_findings.title: Remote Rendering of User-Provided TSX Code
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to send React and Remotion TSX code to the external inference.sh remotion-render app through the belt CLI. This is the core function of the skill, but it can expose proprietary code, props, asset URLs, or other sensitive input to a third-party rendering service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed documentation clearly shows TSX code and render parameters being submitted to inference.sh. The behavior appears intentional and legitimate, but it creates a real data-sharing and remote execution boundary.

low_findings.title: CLI Command Examples Require User Review
low_findings.description: The skill contains examples for installing related skills and running belt commands. These are documented user commands rather than hidden execution, but users should review commands before running them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file contains explicit CLI commands in Markdown, including npx skill installation examples. There is no evidence that these commands execute automatically from the skill package.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Alerts Were False Positives
low_findings.description: The scanner reported weak cryptographic algorithm patterns, but the reviewed lines describe the skill metadata and an input table. No hashing, encryption, or cryptographic code was found in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations contain descriptive Markdown, not cryptographic functions or configuration. This strongly supports dismissing the high static alert as a false positive.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Documentation and Asset URLs
low_findings.description: The skill links to inference.sh, Remotion documentation, and a hosted preview image. These are expected documentation references, but they disclose an external service dependency.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible Markdown links and an image reference. They are not hidden callbacks or exfiltration endpoints, but they confirm network dependency.

dangerous_patterns.title: External CLI Invocation for Remote Render Jobs
dangerous_patterns.description: The documented workflow uses belt app run to call infsh/remotion-render with user-supplied input. This should be treated as an external command and network operation requiring user consent.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command examples directly invoke the belt CLI and submit render input. The pattern is documented and central to the skill behavior.

content.value_statement: Teams need a fast way to turn React animation components into shareable video files. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through rendering Remotion TSX with inference.sh.
actual_capabilities: Shows how to submit Remotion TSX component code for MP4 rendering.
Documents render inputs for duration, FPS, width, height, codec, props, and composition ID.
Provides examples for fade-in text, counters, spring motion, props, and sequences.
Lists supported Remotion and React imports for common animation patterns.
Includes Python SDK examples for render jobs and streaming progress.
limitations: Requires the external inference.sh belt CLI and an authenticated account.
Sends submitted code and props to a remote rendering service.
Does not include local rendering scripts or a bundled Remotion project.
Output quality depends on valid TSX code and supported Remotion APIs.

use_cases.title: Create Product Launch Clips
use_cases.description: Render short branded animations from React components for announcements and social posts.

use_cases.title: Automate Data-Driven Videos
use_cases.description: Generate videos with dynamic props, counters, charts, or text supplied at render time.

use_cases.title: Prototype Motion Concepts
use_cases.description: Test Remotion animation ideas quickly without setting up a full local render pipeline.

prompt_templates.title: Render a Simple Intro
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a basic text animation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a short Remotion intro with fading title text and render it as a 1080p MP4.

prompt_templates.title: Use Custom Props
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request with dynamic title and subtitle values.
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare a Remotion render that accepts title and subtitle props, then render a three-second product update video.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Sequence Animation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Remotion video with three timed sequences, smooth fades, and 1920 by 1080 output at 30 FPS.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Render Workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced production workflow with remote rendering constraints.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Remotion component idea, identify sensitive props to avoid sending remotely, and prepare a safe render plan.

output_examples.input: Render a two-second welcome title with a dark background.
output_examples.output: A short MP4 render plan with fade-in title motion, 1080p dimensions, and 30 FPS settings.

output_examples.input: Create a square animated progress counter for social media.
output_examples.output: A square video render using a large percentage counter, smooth timing, and mobile-friendly dimensions.

output_examples.input: Render a video with title and subtitle values provided at runtime.
output_examples.output: A props-based Remotion render workflow that separates reusable component code from supplied text values.

best_practices: Review TSX code and props before sending them to the remote render service.
Keep render inputs small and explicit, with only the assets needed for the video.
Test short durations first, then increase resolution and duration after the component works.
anti_patterns: Do not include secrets, private tokens, or confidential customer data in render props.
Do not run installation commands without verifying the package source.
Do not use unsupported imports or browser-only APIs without checking Remotion compatibility.

faq.question: What does this skill render?
faq.answer: It renders React and Remotion TSX component code into MP4 video through inference.sh.

faq.question: Does it render locally?
faq.answer: No. The documented workflow uses the external inference.sh belt CLI and remote remotion-render app.

faq.question: Can I pass dynamic values?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill documents a props object that can pass values into the exported component.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report marks it as compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What should I avoid sending?
faq.answer: Avoid secrets, unreleased source code, private asset URLs, and confidential business data.

faq.question: Why is the risk level medium?
faq.answer: The skill is not malicious, but it sends user-provided code and render inputs to an external service.
